honest-test/src/honest_test/numeric.py:1: The Fibonacci sequence has a huge gap near 1_000_000 (832040 then 1346269), so any limit in (832040, 1346269] yields the identical sequence. +1 does not cross a Fibonacci value, so fibonacci_sequence and numeric_values are unchanged. Verified fib(1000000)==fib(1000001).
As above: -1 stays within the (832040, 1346269] gap, so the generated sequence is identical. Verified fib(1000000)==fib(999999).
honest-test/src/honest_test/coverage_dat: indent= controls only JSON whitespace; the serialized document parses to the identical structure, and honest-check reads coverage.json back via json.loads, so the indentation is not part of the cross-tool contract. Verified the parsed object is identical for indent 1/2/3.
As above: indent= is cosmetic whitespace only; the parsed document is identical and honest-check reads it via json.loads.
Every watch-list path is module.attr with exactly one dot, so rsplit('.', 1) and rsplit('.', 2) return the same two parts. No watch-list entry has two dots.
Removing the trailing `return None` in nondeterminism_finding lets the function fall off its end, which returns None implicitly — identical to the explicit return.
honest-test/src/honest_test/honesty.py:s: _name reads link_meta(link).get('name'); @link always sets name == the function __name__, and a non-link callable has no 'name' key (link_meta returns {}). So get('name') is either the __name__ or None, and `or getattr(__name__)` yields the same string whether the key is 'name' or ''. No constructible link makes name differ from __name__.
Emptying the 'server' key makes map_fault_to_http('server') miss the dict and hit the .get default, which is also 500 — the same result. The server category maps to 500 either way.
honest-test/src/honest_test/honesty.py:r: Removing verify_purity's final `return None` (reached only when the two runs are equal) lets the function fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical.
Removing detect_mutation's final `return None` (reached only when the manifest was not mutated) lets the function fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical.
honest-test/src/honest_test/statemachine: Removing the trailing `return None` in _first (reached only when names is empty) lets the function fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical.
honest-test/src/honest_test/mutation.py:: An expression_statement has exactly one child (the inner expression/assignment); trailing comments are not its children. children[0] == children[-1] always. Verified identical across a 20-source battery.

_is_annotation_only's fast-path guard: a non-expression statement's children[0] is never an 'assignment' node, so the assignment check below returns False for it regardless. The guard is an optimisation, not a correctness gate. Verified identical across the battery.

Replacing _is_annotation_only's guard `return False` with pass lets it fall through to the assignment check, which returns False for any non-annotation statement anyway — same result. Verified across the battery.

A `if node.type != X: continue` type guard turned into pass: iterating the wrong node type then hits an inner guard (text not in the swap table, or no matching child), which yields no mutant — so no extra mutant is produced. Verified identical across the battery.
As above: a node-type guard continue->pass is absorbed by the loop body's inner guard, producing no mutant for the wrong node type. Verified identical across the battery.
As above: a node-type guard continue->pass is absorbed by the loop body's inner guard, producing no mutant for the wrong node type. Verified identical across the battery.
Removing _condition_node's trailing `return None` (reached for a non-conditional node) lets it fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical.

Removing the <2-keys guard in _dict_key_swaps is harmless: the cyclic sibling of a sole key is the key itself, and the equal-text check skips that swap, so a dict with fewer than two keys produces no mutant with or without the guard. Verified identical across the battery.

`len(keys) < 2` -> `< 1` only changes empty-dict handling: a 1-key dict (the only newly-admitted case) still produces no mutant because its cyclic sibling is itself and the equal-text check skips it. Verified identical across the battery. (`< 3` would skip 2-key dicts and is caught.)

Removing the node-type guard lets the loop body run for every node; a non-comparison node's children are not comparison-operator tokens, so the inner `text not in _COMPARISON_SWAP` guard yields no mutant. Verified identical across the battery.

Removing the node-type guard lets the loop body run for every node; a non-dictionary node has no child_by_field_name('key'), so keys is empty and the len<2 guard skips it. Verified identical across the battery.
